Major pottery traditions in other prefectures (Arita kilns, Hasami, Agano-yaki in Fukuoka, Satsuma-yaki in Kagoshima, Koishiwara-yaki in Fukuoka, Onta-yaki in Oita) are entirely absent. The see_also venues are mostly general museums and not pottery kilns/workshops. The response itself flags the coverage gap via routing_hint suggesting get_traditional_arts. Hakata Ori (weaving) is included but irrelevant to pottery. Factual accuracy is good for what's present, with one minor note that prefecture_codes for Karatsu includes '24' (Mie) which appears to be a data error.
missing_data: Pottery traditions from other Kyushu prefectures: Koishiwara-yaki and Agano-yaki (Fukuoka), Onta-yaki (Oita), Satsuma-yaki (Kagoshima), Amakusa porcelain (Kumamoto), Hasami-yaki (Nagasaki). Actual kiln/workshop visit information (addresses, tour availability, hours) is absent.
wrong_data: Karatsu Ware prefecture_codes includes '24' (Mie Prefecture) which appears erroneous — Karatsu production is in Saga/Nagasaki area. Hakata Ori (weaving) is irrelevant to the pottery query.
improvement_hint: Apply region_fanout for Kyushu (prefectures 40-47) when the query toponym is 九州/Kyushu, and filter industry_category to 陶磁器 to exclude irrelevant crafts like weaving. Also surface kiln-visit venues via a pottery-specific Wikidata query.

reasoning: The user asked about traditional festivals in the Tohoku region (도호쿠 지방), which covers 6 prefectures (Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Akita, Yamagata, Fukushima). The tool was called only for Aomori, returning just 2 Aomori-specific festivals. The national_heritage section is dominated by non-Tohoku festivals (Saitama, Toyama, Mie, Aichi, Gifu, Chiba, Oita, Tochigi) which are irrelevant noise. Major Tohoku festivals like Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto, Yamagata Hanagasa, and Morioka Sansa are entirely absent. The Korean language requirement (lang=ko) was respected, so RULE D does not apply. Per RULE C, the 2 Aomori items are real authoritative data but the coverage gap is severe — only 1 of 6 prefectures covered. The national_heritage noise further reduces practical usefulness. Failure category G (Coverage Failure) is primary.
missing_data: Festivals from Iwate, Miyagi, Akita, Yamagata, Fukushima prefectures — e.g. Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto Matsuri, Yamagata Hanagasa Matsuri, Morioka Sansa Odori, Fukushima Waraji Matsuri. The region_fanout_applied=false confirms no fan-out was attempted.
wrong_data: The national_heritage section includes many non-Tohoku festivals (Saitama, Toyama, Mie, Aichi, Gifu, Chiba, Oita, Tochigi) presented alongside Aomori results, which is misleading for a Tohoku-focused query.
improvement_hint: When prefecture is a single Tohoku prefecture but the query intent is clearly regional (도호쿠 지방), the server should apply region_fanout to all 6 Tohoku prefectures. Also, national_heritage items should be filtered to Tohoku-relevant entries only, not a national dump of unrelated festivals.
